title: Differential-Geometry-Guided Adversarial Negative Mining for EqM (DG-ANM)

stages.A: Proxy sweep on 2ep IN-100 + 3-seed repeatability gate (CURRENT)
stages.A.5: Reproduce vanilla EqM on CIFAR-10 at paper FID 3.36 (stack reproducibility gate; our current CIFAR 80ep gets FID 497, which is a bug)
stages.B: IN-100 (same subset as prior 80ep runs: vanilla FID 121.24, DG-ANM 112.58 at 1 seed each) class-conditional, EqM-B/2, 80ep, DG-ANM vs vanilla EqM (3 seeds each, 50K FID). This is the paper's primary benchmark.
stages.C: Scaling curves (S/2, B/2, L/2 on IN-100 (same subset as prior 80ep runs: vanilla FID 121.24, DG-ANM 112.58 at 1 seed each)) + CIFAR-10 secondary result (3 seeds, appendix-style)
stages.D: Mechanism analyses + ablation tables + negative-mining baseline comparisons

publication_goal.stage_a_exit_gate: best proxy config must beat vanilla-EqM baseline by >=1 FID across 3 seeds; if not, do NOT scale up — reformulate
publication_goal.stage_a5_exit_gate: vanilla EqM on CIFAR-10 (paper recipe, Appendix B.1) must reach FID ~3.36 (within 0.3); if not, fix the stack before any Stage B compute

reference_numbers.eqm_paper_cifar10_note: EqM is WORSE than Flow Matching (2.09) on CIFAR; CIFAR is an appendix ablation, NOT the headline benchmark

next_action.command: Execute Stage A.5 Step B (DDPM UNet smoke test) and, in parallel, start Stage B IN-100 (same subset as prior 80ep runs: vanilla FID 121.24, DG-ANM 112.58 at 1 seed each) vanilla baseline training.
next_action.reason: User chose path B->A (compute not tight). Step B: diffusers UNet2DModel on CIFAR-10 with plain FM/DDPM loss, same data + eval pipeline as ours, target FID <15 in a few hours to confirm architecture is the dominant bug. In parallel: start the long-running Stage B vanilla EqM-B/2 IN-100 (same subset as prior 80ep runs: vanilla FID 121.24, DG-ANM 112.58 at 1 seed each) 80ep baseline on the known-working transformer stack.
next_action.hint: Plan doc: documentation/stage-a5-plan.md. Step B exit criterion: FID<15 -> proceed to Step A (port FM UNet + graft EqM loss, ~1 week). Step A exit criterion: vanilla EqM CIFAR FID <=3.66 (paper 3.36 + 0.3 tolerance). Stage B vanilla IN-256 baseline training is the biggest calendar win available.

update_2026_04_18_1610.stage_a5_step_b_result: PASSED: cifar10_smoke_fid=9.7193 on 10K samples (exit criterion was FID<15)
update_2026_04_18_1610.interpretation: Architecture confirmed as dominant cause of CIFAR FID 497 bug. UNet + plain FM loss reached FID 9.72 with our same data + FID eval pipelines, vs our broken transformer's FID 497 (51x reduction). Step A (port FM UNet + EqM loss) is unblocked and is the next concrete work item.
update_2026_04_18_1610.pi_update_trigger_fired: stage_exit_gate_pass — draft PI update per documentation/pi-updates.md
next_concrete_actions: 1. Draft PI update entry in documentation/pi-updates.md (Stage A.5 gate passed)
2. Queue Step A implementation: vendor facebookresearch/flow_matching, port CIFAR UNet, graft EqM c(gamma)-weighted loss, train vanilla to FID<=3.66
3. Wait for Stage B array to start (still PENDING on seas_gpu)
4. Round 4 c1 still running but likely to timeout (at step 12400/16174 with ~35m walltime left); c8 lr=2e-4 at FID 249.33 is the provisional round 4 winner

needs_user_input.prompt: Stage A.5 gate PASSED (CIFAR smoke FID 9.72 < 15). Draft PI update is ready in documentation/pi-updates.md — please review and send. After that, do you want me to start Step A (port FM UNet + graft EqM loss) now, or wait until Round 4 finishes?

stage_a5.step_b_result: UNet + plain FM loss on CIFAR-10 reached FID 9.72 on 10K samples, vs our broken transformer's FID 497. 18x improvement from architecture change alone confirms architecture was the dominant cause. Step A (port FM UNet + graft EqM loss) unblocked.
stage_a5.step_b_exit_criterion: CIFAR-10 FID < 15 with off-the-shelf UNet + plain FM/DDPM loss + our data & eval pipelines
stage_a5.step_a_exit_criterion: vanilla EqM on CIFAR-10 via ported FM UNet reaches FID <= 3.66 (paper 3.36 + 0.3 tolerance), 50K samples, paper-matching sampler
stage_a5.parallel_track: Stage B IN-100 (same subset as prior 80ep runs: vanilla FID 121.24, DG-ANM 112.58 at 1 seed each) vanilla EqM-B/2 80ep baseline can start now on the known-working transformer stack (independent of Stage A.5)

active_runs.description: Stage A.5 Step A: vanilla EqM on CIFAR-10 via FM UNet. 1800 epochs bs=64 lr=2e-4. Exit: FID<=3.66 on 50K samples (paper 3.36 +0.3 tol). Replaces cancelled job 6497535 (UNet extra-arg bug).

Stage A.5 Step A (parallel DG-ANM): DG-ANM + EqM on CIFAR-10 via FM UNet. Same backbone/loss as vanilla, plus feature-space kNN tangent/normal decomposition + PGA mining + margin loss. Hyperparams from IN-100 proxy winners: gamma=6.0, eps=0.8, mining_steps=3, mine_every=5, neg_margin=5.0. Exploratory — transfer from 2ep IN-100 transformer to 1800ep CIFAR UNet is not guaranteed.
Stage B vanilla EqM-B/2 IN-100 80ep seed 0 (array 6467702, baseline for DG-ANM vs vanilla 3-seed comparison)
Stage B vanilla EqM-B/2 IN-100 80ep seed 1 (array 6467702)
Stage B vanilla EqM-B/2 IN-100 80ep seed 2 (array 6467702)
Round 4 candidate 1: mine_every=1 (most aggressive). At risk of timeout (step 12400/16174 with ~35m walltime left at 1.02 steps/sec; needs ~60m more).

completed_runs.reason: UNet extra-arg bug (FM UNet requires extra={} positional; my wrapper was added in commit c8a5f7b). Replaced by job 6499055 on fixed code.

completed_runs.interpretation: Architecture (UNet vs transformer) confirmed as dominant cause of CIFAR FID 497 bug. 18x reduction from transformer's 497 to UNet's 9.72. Stage A.5 Step A (port FM UNet + graft EqM loss) is now unblocked.

imagenet_evaluation.conclusion: DG-ANM improves FID by 7.1% at 80 epochs (121.24 → 112.58). Mining adds ~2x training time but produces better generative quality.

events.detail: Round 6: C3 wins (0.003685). bs=256 (later invalidated — OOM on MIG).
Round 7: all 5 OOM on 20GB MIG GPUs with bs=256.
Round 9: C2 wins (0.003458). lr=1e-5. Best proxy metric.
CIFAR-10 FID ~450-500 for all models. Pixel-space sampling broken.
ImageNet-100 downloaded (117K images, 100 classes). EqM-B/2 pipeline built.
DG-ANM EqM-B/2 80-epoch training complete. FID 112.58. 7.1% improvement.
